name: Running from Lorxa by the Vía Verde del Serpis
name_original: Running desde Lorxa por la Vía Verde del Serpis
region_intro: Jogging routes around Lorcha/Orxa traverse a diverse landscape of picturesque mountains, lush valleys, and the prominent Serpis River. The region features varied terrain, from tranquil riparian paths along the river to more challenging ascents in areas like Cim de la Safor. Many routes utilize the Via Verde del Serpis, offering both accessible and unpaved sections for runners. This area provides a mix of natural features, including dramatic gorges and verdant scenery, suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Lorcha/Orxa The most popular jogging route is Running from Lorxa by the Vía Verde del Serpis, a 12.3 miles (19.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route follows the historic greenway, offering a mix of accessible and unpaved sections along the Serpis River. Another top favourite among local runners is Cova de les Rates loop from Arenal de Palmer, a difficult 10.2 miles (16.3 km) path. This route navigates through mountainous terrain, providing a strenuous workout with significant elevation changes. Local runners also love the Cim de la Safor loop from l'Orxa / Lorcha, a 10.4 miles (16.7 km) trail leading through Cim de la Safor, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. This challenging route offers rewarding ascents and panoramic views. Jogging around Lorcha/Orxa is defined by the Serpis River, the Via Verde, and the surrounding mountains and valleys, including the Vall de la Gallinera. The network offers options for different ability levels, from accessible paths to demanding trail runs. The routes in Lorcha/Orxa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Lorcha/Orxa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: There are over 60 dedicated running routes around Lorcha/Orxa, offering a diverse range of experiences for all fitness levels. These include 23 moderate and 41 difficult routes, ensuring options from scenic riverside jogs to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes for beginners in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: While the guide primarily lists moderate to difficult routes, the region does offer accessible paths suitable for beginners. Sections of the &lt;strong&gt;Via Verde del Serpis&lt;/strong&gt; are generally easier, providing a pleasant jogging experience through diverse scenery. You can find routes that follow the Serpis River for a more gentle run.

Q: What are the most challenging running routes in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: For those seeking a strenuous workout, Lorcha/Orxa offers several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21951420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cim de la Safor loop from l'Orxa / Lorcha&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult 16.7 km trail with significant elevation changes, leading through Cim de la Safor and offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33803992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de les Rates loop from Arenal de Palmer&lt;/a&gt;, which navigates through mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21951420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cim de la Safor loop from l'Orxa / Lorcha&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33803992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de les Rates loop from Arenal de Palmer&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular choices for runners.

Q: What scenic views or natural features can I expect on a run in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Lorcha/Orxa boasts dramatic and verdant scenery, with the &lt;strong&gt;Serpis River&lt;/strong&gt; as a central feature. You can enjoy tranquil riparian landscapes, especially along the Via Verde del Serpis. The region also features impressive natural monuments, mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alt de Benicadell&lt;/a&gt;, and canyons such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4155453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallinera Valley&lt;/a&gt;. In spring, the Vall de la Gallinera is particularly beautiful with cherry blossoms.

Q: Are there routes that follow the Serpis River?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;strong&gt;Serpis River&lt;/strong&gt; is a prominent natural feature, and many routes, including the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872830942/running-from-lorxa-by-the-via-verde-del-serpis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running from Lorxa by the Vía Verde del Serpis&lt;/a&gt;, allow joggers to follow its course. These paths offer tranquil sounds and beautiful riparian landscapes, though some sections along the old railway line can be unpaved and gravelly.

Q: Can I find routes with historical landmarks or points of interest?
A: Yes, the region combines natural beauty with historical charm. For instance, the charming Forna Village, nestled among orange groves, features the impressive &lt;strong&gt;Castillo de Forna&lt;/strong&gt;, a fortified palace that adds a historical landmark to a scenic jog. You can also explore areas around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/486065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Greenway of the Serpis River&lt;/a&gt; which has historical significance as an old railway line.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: The running routes in Lorcha/Orxa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from accessible paths along the Serpis River to demanding trail runs through mountains and valleys, and the stunning natural backdrop of the region.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or gorges to see on the running trails?
A: Yes, the area features impressive natural formations. You can find gorges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4155453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallinera Valley&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2897664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll de l’Estret&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2599324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg del Salt de Planes&lt;/a&gt; is a notable waterfall that can be a rewarding sight during your run.

Q: Are the jogging routes in Lorcha/Orxa suitable for families?
A: While the guide lists many moderate to difficult routes, some sections of the &lt;strong&gt;Via Verde del Serpis&lt;/strong&gt; are more accessible and could be suitable for families, especially those with older children or those looking for a less strenuous outing. It's advisable to check the specific route details for terrain and elevation to ensure it matches your family's fitness level.

Q: Can I jog with my dog on the trails in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: Many natural trails in the Lorcha/Orxa region are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy a run with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near roads, and to respect local regulations regarding pets in natural parks or protected areas. Ensure your dog is fit for the chosen route's distance and terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Lorcha/Orxa?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for running for much of the year. Spring is particularly beautiful, especially in the &lt;strong&gt;Vall de la Gallinera&lt;/strong&gt; when cherry blossoms are in bloom. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended, while winters are generally mild, allowing for comfortable jogging.
tips: The Via Verde runs along the Serpis River and is one of the most colorful and entertaining routes in the Valencian Community. Following the old railway route that has now been converted, you will find waterfalls, weirs, old railway buildings and tunnels that make for a very entertaining tour.
